commands.review_summary:
The PR makes `renderMarkdownIRChunksWithinLimit` treat `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY` as an unbounded chunk limit and adds a regression test for that sentinel.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +14. Total +21 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main normalizes `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY` to `1`, and the Signal media path then sends only the first chunk as the caption. I did not run a live Signal send in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This PR should stay in normal review until contributor proof and maintainer compatibility acceptance are present; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to queue.

target.title: fix(markdown-core): treat Infinity chunk limit as unbounded, not 1
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92734. Sending a Signal image **with a caption** delivered only the caption's **first character** (`"Here's another photo…"` → `"H"`).

`renderMarkdownIRChunksWithinLimit` normalized its `limit` via `resolveIntegerOption(limit, 1, { min: 1 })`, which rejects non-finite values and falls back to `1`. Callers that pass `Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY` to mean "no size cap" (Signal media captions, and the Signal text path when the chunk limit is disabled) therefore got a limit of `1`, splitting the message into one chunk per character; the caller takes `[0]`, so only the first letter survived.

This emits the whole IR as a single rendered chunk when the limit is `Infinity`. `NaN` and other invalid limits keep the existing fallback, and finite limits still split exactly as before — so the change is scoped to the "no cap" sentinel.

## Test Plan

- Added a regression test in `render-aware-chunking.test.ts`: an `Infinity` limit returns a single chunk containing the full text. The existing `NaN` fallback test is left intact and still passes (verifies invalid limits are unchanged).
- Validated the edited source directly against the reported caption:
  - `limit: Infinity` → `1` chunk, full text `"Here's another photo of Ethan from Miyakojima, Japan on February 23, 2026."`
  - `limit: NaN` (`"abc"`) → `["a","b","c"]` (fallback unchanged)
  - `limit: 20` → `5` chunks (finite splitting unchanged)
- Not run locally: the full vitest suite / lint / typecheck (contributor clone without a full workspace install). CI covers these.
